arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca has three weeks to save Chelsea season amid top four crisis and sack pressure
arts.sum: Yet, close your other eye, turn the head again, and Chelsea could easily find themselves only once place behind Arsenal by the time they have played two more league games. For 20 minutes against Aston Villa, Chelsea showed the fight and grit at a Tough Place To Go of a side who, in contrast to previous weeks, actually felt and played like they could still make something of this season and it wasn't all happening to them.

arts.ttl: What Cole Palmer did to wow Enzo Maresca as transfer question asked over Chelsea star
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has continued to back Cole Palmer with the Chelsea attacker going through a bit of a drought in front of goal. With 39 goals and 21 assists in 74 appearances across all competitions, there has become a reliance on Palmer to perform from a Blues' point of view - something Maresca spoke about on Monday morning, suggesting there was too much responsibility on the England international's shoulders.

arts.ttl: 'We will see': Maresca makes SHOCK admission over Palmer Chelsea future
arts.sum: Enzo Maresca has hinted that Cole Palmer could face an uncertain future at Chelsea if they fail to qualify for the Champions League this season. Despite Palmer renewing his contract in August until 2033, speculation has been rife over his future amid reports that the 22-year-old’s deal contains a release clause if Chelsea do not qualify for the Champions League.

arts.ttl: Report: In-demand Chelsea target who shackled Alexander Isak last month wants summer transfer
arts.sum: Limited Isak to just one shot!

arts.ttl: Chelsea vs Southampton announcement sees surprise name nobody will have heard of take centre stage
arts.sum: The referee for the clash at Stamford Bridge is a man named Tom Bramall and he will not be that familiar to many football fans in the Premier League . It will be interesting for the fans to see how Bramall gets on in a big match in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Palmer dropped, Mudryk replacement starts, midfield twist - Chelsea lineups vs Southampton - football.london
arts.sum: Tom's XI: Jorgensen; Acheampong, Tosin, Colwill, Cucurella; Fernandez, Caicedo, Dyer; George, Neto, Sancho. Chelsea shouldn't need a rest after coming off the back of a free midweek and facing another seven days without match action, Maresca could still ring the changes.
